volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 1166 - Section 1.1269)

sections.label: Section 1166
sections.original: νὴ τοὺς θεοὺς ἔγωγε · καὶ γὰρ ἄξιον ·
sections.translation: By the gods , yes , and with good reason . It ' s really not to be believed . But here comes another messenger from the wall to bring us some further news ! What a fighting look he has !

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: ἰοὺ ἰού , ἰοὺ ἰού , ἰοὺ ἰού .
sections.translation: Alas ! alas ! alas ! alas ! alas ! alas !

sections.label: Section 1171
sections.translation: What ' s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 1171b
sections.translation: A horrible outrage has occurred ; a god sent by Zeus has passed through our gates and has penetrated the realms of the air without the knowledge of the jays , who are on guard in the daytime .

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original: ὦ δεινὸν ἔργον καὶ σχέτλιον εἰργασμένος .
sections.translation: It ' s a terrible and criminal deed . What god was it ?

sections.label: Section 1176b
sections.original: οὐκ ἴσμεν · ὅτι δʼ εἶχε πτερά ,
sections.translation: We don ' t know that . All we know is , that he has got wings .

sections.label: Section 1177b
sections.translation: Why were not patrolmen sent against him at once ?

sections.label: Section 1178b
sections.translation: We have despatched thirty thousand hawks of the legion of Mounted Archers .

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: χωρεῖ δὲ πᾶς τις ὄνυχας ἠγκυλωμένος ,
sections.translation: All the hook-clawed birds are moving against him , the kestrel , the buzzard , the vulture , the great-horned owl ; they cleave the air so that it resounds with the flapping of their wings ; they are looking everywhere for the god , who cannot be far away ; indeed , if I mistake not ,

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.translation: he is coming from yonder side .

sections.label: Section 1185b
sections.translation: To arms , all , with slings and bows ! This way , all our soldiers ; shoot and strike ! Some one give me a sling !

sections.label: Section 1.1189
sections.translation: War , a terrible war is breaking out

sections.label: Section 1.1190
sections.original: πρὸς ἐμὲ καὶ θεούς . ἀλλὰ φύλαττε πᾶς
sections.translation: between us and the gods ! Come , let each one guard Air , the son of Erebus , in which the clouds float .

sections.label: Section 1.1195
sections.original: μή σε λάθῃ θεῶν τις ταύτῃ περῶν ·
sections.translation: Take care no immortal enters it without your knowledge .

sections.label: Section 1.1196
sections.translation: Scan all sides with your glance . Hark ! methinks I can hear the rustle of the swift wings of a god from heaven .

sections.label: Section 1.1199
sections.original: αὕτη σύ , ποῖ ποῖ ποῖ πέτει ; μένʼ ἥσυχος ,
sections.translation: Hi ! you woman ! where , where , where are you flying to ? Halt , don ' t stir !

sections.label: Section 1.1200
sections.original: ἔχʼ ἀτρέμας · αὐτοῦ στῆθʼ · ἐπίσχες τοῦ δρόμου .
sections.translation: keep motionless ! not a beat of your wing ! She pauses in her flight . Who are you and from what country ? You must say whence you come .

sections.label: Section 1.1202
sections.original: παρὰ τῶν θεῶν ἔγωγε τῶν Ὀλυμπίων .
sections.translation: I come from the abode of the Olympian gods .

sections.label: Section 1.1203
sections.original: ὄνομα δέ σο . τί ἐστι ; πλοῖον ἢ κυνῆ ;
sections.translation: What ' s your name , ship or head-dress ?

sections.label: Section 1.1204
sections.translation: I am swift Iris .

sections.label: Section 1.1204b
sections.translation: Paralus or Salaminia ?

sections.label: Section 1.1205
sections.translation: What do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 1.1205b
sections.translation: Let a buzzard rush at her and seize her .

sections.label: Section 1.1206b
sections.translation: Seize me ? But what do all these insults mean ?

sections.label: Section 1.1207b
sections.translation: Woe to you !

sections.label: Section 1.1208
sections.translation: I do not understand it .

sections.label: Section 1.1208b
sections.translation: By which gate did you pass through the wall , wretched woman ?

sections.label: Section 1.1210
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα μὰ Δίʼ ἔγωγε κατὰ ποίας πύλας .
sections.translation: By which gate ? Why , great gods , I don ' t know .

sections.label: Section 1.1211
sections.translation: You hear how she holds us in derision . Did you present yourself to the officers in command of the jays ? You don ' t answer . Have you a permit , bearing the seal of the storks ?

sections.label: Section 1.1213b
sections.translation: Am I dreaming ?

sections.label: Section 1.1214
sections.translation: Did you get one ?

sections.label: Section 1.1214b
sections.translation: Are you mad ?

sections.label: Section 1.1214c
sections.translation: No

sections.label: Section 1.1215
sections.translation: head-bird gave you a safe-conduct ?

sections.label: Section 1.1216
sections.original: μὰ Δίʼ οὐκ ἔμοιγʼ ἐπέβαλεν οὐδεὶς ὦ μέλε .
sections.translation: A safe-conduct to me . You poor fool !

sections.label: Section 1.1217
sections.translation: Ah ! and so you slipped into this city on the sly and into these realms of air-land that don ' t belong to you .

sections.label: Section 1.1219
sections.original: ποίᾳ γὰρ ἄλλῃ χρὴ πέτεσθαι τοὺς θεούς ;
sections.translation: And what other roads can the gods travel ?

sections.label: Section 1.1220
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα μὰ Δίʼ ἔγωγε · τῇδε μὲν γὰρ οὔ .
sections.translation: By Zeus ! I know nothing about that , not I . But they won ' t pass this way . And you still dare to complain ? Why , if you were treated according to your deserts , no Iris would ever have more justly suffered death .

sections.label: Section 1.1224
sections.translation: I am immortal .

sections.label: Section 1.1224b
sections.translation: You would have died nevertheless . —

sections.label: Section 1.1225
sections.original: δεινότατα γάρ τοι πεισόμεσθʼ , ἐμοὶ δοκεῖ ,
sections.translation: Oh ! that would be truly intolerable ! What ! should the universe obey us and the gods alone continue their insolence and not understand that they must submit to the law of the strongest in their due turn ? But tell me , where are you flying to ?

sections.label: Section 1.1230
sections.original: ἐγώ ; πρὸς ἀνθρώπους πέτομαι παρὰ τοῦ πατρὸς
sections.translation: I ? The messenger of Zeus to mankind , I am going to tell them to sacrifice sheep and oxen on the altars and to fill their streets with the rich smoke of burning fat .

sections.label: Section 1.1233b
sections.translation: Of which gods are you speaking ?

sections.label: Section 1.1234
sections.original: ποίοισιν ; ἡμῖν τοῖς ἐν οὐρανῷ θεοῖς .
sections.translation: Of which ? Why , of ourselves , the gods of heaven .

sections.label: Section 1.1235
sections.translation: You , gods ?

sections.label: Section 1.1235b
sections.translation: Are there others then ?

sections.label: Section 1.1236
sections.translation: Men now adore the birds as gods , and it ' s to them , by Zeus , that they must offer sacrifices , and not to Zeus at all !

sections.label: Section 1.1238
sections.original: ὦ μῶρε μῶρε μὴ θεῶν κίνει φρένας
sections.translation: Oh ! fool ! fool ! Rouse not

sections.label: Section 1.1240
sections.translation: the wrath of the gods , for it is terrible indeed . Armed with the brand of Zeus , Justice would annihilate your race ; the lightning would strike you as it did Licymnius and consume both your body and the porticos of your palace .

sections.label: Section 1.1243
sections.translation: Here ! that ' s enough tall talk . Just you listen and keep quiet ! Do you take me for a Lydian or a Phrygian

sections.label: Section 1.1245
sections.translation: and think to frighten me with your big words ? Know , that if Zeus worries me again , I shall go at the head of my eagles , who are armed with lightning , and reduce his dwelling and that of Amphion to cinders . I shall send more than six hundred porphyrions

sections.label: Section 1.1250
sections.translation: clothed in leopards ' skins up to heaven against him ; and formerly a single Porphyrion gave him enough to do . As for you , his messenger , if you annoy me , I shall begin by getting between your thighs ,

sections.label: Section 1.1255
sections.original: τὴν Ἶριν αὐτήν , ὥστε θαυμάζειν ὅπως
sections.translation: and even though you are Iris , you will be surprised at the erection the old man can produce ; it ' s three times as good as the ram on a ship ' s prow !

sections.label: Section 1.1257
sections.translation: May you perish , you wretch , you and your infamous words !

sections.label: Section 1.1258
sections.original: οὐκ ἀποσοβήσεις ; οὐ ταχέως ; εὐρὰξ πατάξ .
sections.translation: Won ' t you get out of here quickly ? Come , stretch your wings or look out for squalls !

sections.label: Section 1.1259
sections.original: ἦ μήν σε παύσει τῆς ὕβρεως οὑμὸς πατήρ .
sections.translation: If my father does not punish you for your insults ...

sections.label: Section 1.1260
sections.translation: Ha ! ... but just you be off elsewhere to roast younger folk than us with your lightning .

sections.label: Section 1.1263
sections.translation: We forbid the gods , the sons of Zeus , to pass through our city

sections.label: Section 1.1265
sections.original: μηδέ γέ τινʼ ἱερόθυτον ἀνὰ δάπεδον ἔτι
sections.translation: and the mortals to send them the smoke of their sacrifices by this road .

sections.label: Section 1.1269
sections.original: δεινόν γε τὸν κήρυκα τὸν παρὰ τοὺς βροτοὺς
sections.translation: It ' s odd that the messenger
